name: Northwestern Catalan (Lleidatà)
notes: Northwestern Catalan (Lleidatà/Pallarès). Western block, spoken in Lleida and Pyrenean valleys. NO unstressed vowel reduction: the 7-vowel system is fully maintained in unstressed positions (unlike Eastern/Central Catalan), so unstressed /a e o/ do NOT reduce to [ə]/[u] (Recasens 1996; Veny 1982). This is modelled by overriding the inherited Central ⟨nucleus_unstressed⟩ reduction: the vowel graphemes declare only a full-quality ⟨default⟩, so both stressed and unstressed nuclei keep [a], [ɛ]/[e], [ɔ]/[o]. Palatal lateral /ʎ/ preserved without yeísmo. Rhotic /ɾ/ may be trilled [r] in some contexts (word-initial, post-nasal). Intervocalic spirantization and final devoicing (inherited allophone_rules) still apply. Closest to Valencian in vowel system.

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages

sources.author: Recasens, D.
sources.title: Fonètica descriptiva del català
sources.notes: Nord-Occidental (Western) Catalan vocalism: unstressed vowels are NOT reduced to schwa; the tonic 7-vowel inventory is preserved in atonic position, unlike the Eastern/Central varieties.

sources.author: Veny, J.
sources.title: Els parlars catalans: síntesi de dialectologia
sources.notes: Dialectological isogloss separating Western (no atonic reduction) from Eastern (atonic reduction to [ə]/[u]) Catalan.
